description: SQL Q&A pairs for few-shot training - DuckDB compatible

examples.question: Show each entity's initial and latest measurement values
examples.sql:
WITH RankedMeasurements AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        measurement_date,
        value,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY measurement_date ASC) AS first_rank,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY measurement_date DESC) AS last_rank
    FROM measurements
    WHERE value IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    f.entity_id,
    f.value AS initial_value,
    l.value AS latest_value
FROM RankedMeasurements f
JOIN RankedMeasurements l ON f.entity_id = l.entity_id
WHERE f.first_rank = 1 AND l.last_rank = 1;
examples.description: Pattern for comparing first vs last chronological values using ROW_NUMBER

examples.question: Compare the first and most recent status for each record
examples.sql:
WITH StatusRanked AS (
    SELECT 
        record_id,
        status,
        recorded_at,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY record_id ORDER BY recorded_at ASC) AS asc_rank,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY record_id ORDER BY recorded_at DESC) AS desc_rank
    FROM status_history
)
SELECT 
    first_rec.record_id,
    first_rec.status AS first_status,
    first_rec.recorded_at AS first_date,
    last_rec.status AS last_status,
    last_rec.recorded_at AS last_date
FROM StatusRanked first_rec
JOIN StatusRanked last_rec ON first_rec.record_id = last_rec.record_id
WHERE first_rec.asc_rank = 1 AND last_rec.desc_rank = 1;
examples.description: Self-join pattern to get both first and last records in one query

examples.question: Find records where the value increased from the previous entry
examples.sql:
WITH ValueChanges AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        entry_date,
        value,
        LAG(value) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY entry_date) AS prev_value
    FROM entries
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    entry_date,
    value,
    prev_value,
    value - prev_value AS increase
FROM ValueChanges
WHERE prev_value IS NOT NULL AND value > prev_value;
examples.description: Use CTE to compute LAG, then filter in outer query - never use window functions in WHERE

examples.question: Identify entries that differ from the next scheduled entry
examples.sql:
WITH WithNext AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        category,
        scheduled_date,
        LEAD(category) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY scheduled_date) AS next_category,
        LEAD(scheduled_date) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY scheduled_date) AS next_date
    FROM schedule
)
SELECT 
    id,
    category,
    scheduled_date,
    next_category,
    next_date
FROM WithNext
WHERE next_category IS NOT NULL AND category != next_category;
examples.description: LEAD function in CTE to look ahead, filter changes in outer query

examples.question: Find consecutive periods where status remained the same
examples.sql:
WITH Ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        status,
        record_date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY record_date) AS overall_rank,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id, status ORDER BY record_date) AS status_rank
    FROM records
),
StreakGroups AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        status,
        record_date,
        overall_rank - status_rank AS streak_group
    FROM Ranked
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    status,
    streak_group,
    COUNT(*) AS streak_length,
    MIN(record_date) AS streak_start,
    MAX(record_date) AS streak_end
FROM StreakGroups
GROUP BY entity_id, status, streak_group
ORDER BY entity_id, streak_start;
examples.description: ROW_NUMBER difference technique to identify consecutive sequences

examples.question: Count consecutive days with activity for each user
examples.sql:
WITH DailyActivity AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        user_id,
        CAST(activity_timestamp AS DATE) AS activity_date
    FROM activities
),
Ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        activity_date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY activity_date) AS seq_num,
        activity_date - INTERVAL '1 day' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY activity_date) AS streak_base
    FROM DailyActivity
)
SELECT 
    user_id,
    MIN(activity_date) AS streak_start,
    MAX(activity_date) AS streak_end,
    COUNT(*) AS consecutive_days
FROM Ranked
GROUP BY user_id, streak_base
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY consecutive_days DESC;
examples.description: Date-based streak detection using row number subtraction from date

examples.question: Group events into episodes with gaps of more than 30 days
examples.sql:
WITH WithGaps AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        event_date,
        LAG(event_date) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY event_date) AS prev_date
    FROM events
),
EpisodeStarts AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        event_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN prev_date IS NULL THEN 1
            WHEN CAST(event_date AS DATE) - CAST(prev_date AS DATE) > 30 THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS is_new_episode
    FROM WithGaps
),
EpisodeNumbers AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        event_date,
        SUM(is_new_episode) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY event_date) AS episode_num
    FROM EpisodeStarts
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    episode_num,
    MIN(event_date) AS episode_start,
    MAX(event_date) AS episode_end,
    COUNT(*) AS events_in_episode
FROM EpisodeNumbers
GROUP BY entity_id, episode_num
ORDER BY entity_id, episode_num;
examples.description: Gap-based episode detection using running sum of episode start flags

examples.question: Identify visit episodes separated by 90 or more days
examples.sql:
WITH VisitGaps AS (
    SELECT 
        client_id,
        visit_date,
        LAG(visit_date) OVER (PARTITION BY client_id ORDER BY visit_date) AS prev_visit
    FROM visits
),
NewEpisodeFlags AS (
    SELECT 
        client_id,
        visit_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN prev_visit IS NULL THEN 1
            WHEN DATEDIFF('day', prev_visit, visit_date) >= 90 THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS new_episode_flag
    FROM VisitGaps
),
EpisodeAssigned AS (
    SELECT 
        client_id,
        visit_date,
        SUM(new_episode_flag) OVER (PARTITION BY client_id ORDER BY visit_date) AS episode_id
    FROM NewEpisodeFlags
)
SELECT 
    client_id,
    episode_id,
    COUNT(*) AS visit_count,
    MIN(visit_date) AS first_visit,
    MAX(visit_date) AS last_visit,
    DATEDIFF('day', MIN(visit_date), MAX(visit_date)) AS episode_duration_days
FROM EpisodeAssigned
GROUP BY client_id, episode_id;
examples.description: Episode grouping with 90-day gap threshold using DATEDIFF

examples.question: Find outliers using z-score greater than 2 standard deviations
examples.sql:
WITH Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(value) AS mean_val,
        STDDEV(value) AS stddev_val
    FROM measurements
    WHERE value IS NOT NULL
),
WithZScore AS (
    SELECT 
        m.entity_id,
        m.measurement_date,
        m.value,
        s.mean_val,
        s.stddev_val,
        CASE 
            WHEN s.stddev_val > 0 THEN (m.value - s.mean_val) / s.stddev_val
            ELSE 0
        END AS z_score
    FROM measurements m
    CROSS JOIN Stats s
    WHERE m.value IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    measurement_date,
    value,
    ROUND(z_score, 2) AS z_score
FROM WithZScore
WHERE ABS(z_score) > 2
ORDER BY ABS(z_score) DESC;
examples.description: Z-score calculation using CTE for aggregates - never put AVG/STDDEV in WHERE

examples.question: Calculate percentile ranks for each value within groups
examples.sql:
WITH GroupStats AS (
    SELECT 
        group_id,
        COUNT(*) AS group_count,
        AVG(score) AS avg_score,
        MIN(score) AS min_score,
        MAX(score) AS max_score
    FROM scores
    GROUP BY group_id
),
WithRanks AS (
    SELECT 
        s.entity_id,
        s.group_id,
        s.score,
        gs.avg_score,
        gs.group_count,
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY s.group_id ORDER BY s.score) AS percentile_rank
    FROM scores s
    JOIN GroupStats gs ON s.group_id = gs.group_id
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    group_id,
    score,
    ROUND(avg_score, 2) AS group_avg,
    ROUND(percentile_rank * 100, 1) AS percentile
FROM WithRanks
WHERE percentile_rank >= 0.9
ORDER BY group_id, percentile_rank DESC;
examples.description: Percentile calculation with group statistics using CTEs

examples.question: Find records from the last 90 days
examples.sql:
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    record_date,
    value
FROM records
WHERE record_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days'
ORDER BY record_date DESC;
examples.description: DuckDB date subtraction using INTERVAL syntax - not DATE_SUB

examples.question: Calculate days between first and last event for each entity
examples.sql:
WITH DateRange AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        MIN(event_date) AS first_event,
        MAX(event_date) AS last_event
    FROM events
    GROUP BY entity_id
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    first_event,
    last_event,
    DATEDIFF('day', first_event, last_event) AS days_between,
    CAST(last_event AS DATE) - CAST(first_event AS DATE) AS days_alt
FROM DateRange
WHERE first_event != last_event
ORDER BY days_between DESC;
examples.description: DuckDB DATEDIFF requires 3 arguments: unit, start, end

examples.question: Calculate 7-day trailing average for each entity
examples.sql:
WITH DailyValues AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        CAST(recorded_at AS DATE) AS record_date,
        AVG(value) AS daily_avg
    FROM measurements
    GROUP BY entity_id, CAST(recorded_at AS DATE)
)
SELECT 
    d1.entity_id,
    d1.record_date,
    d1.daily_avg AS current_value,
    AVG(d2.daily_avg) AS trailing_7day_avg,
    COUNT(d2.daily_avg) AS days_in_window
FROM DailyValues d1
LEFT JOIN DailyValues d2 
    ON d1.entity_id = d2.entity_id
    AND d2.record_date BETWEEN d1.record_date - INTERVAL '6 days' AND d1.record_date
GROUP BY d1.entity_id, d1.record_date, d1.daily_avg
HAVING COUNT(d2.daily_avg) >= 3
ORDER BY d1.entity_id, d1.record_date;
examples.description: Trailing average using self-join with date range

examples.question: Compute 30-day moving sum of activities
examples.sql:
WITH DailyCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        CAST(activity_date AS DATE) AS activity_day,
        COUNT(*) AS daily_count
    FROM activities
    GROUP BY entity_id, CAST(activity_date AS DATE)
),
AllDays AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        activity_day,
        daily_count,
        SUM(daily_count) OVER (
            PARTITION BY entity_id 
            ORDER BY activity_day 
            ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS rolling_30day_sum
    FROM DailyCounts
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    activity_day,
    daily_count,
    rolling_30day_sum
FROM AllDays
ORDER BY entity_id, activity_day;
examples.description: Rolling sum using window function with ROWS BETWEEN

examples.question: Find entities whose category changed from first to last record
examples.sql:
WITH Ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        category,
        recorded_at,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY recorded_at ASC) AS first_rank,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY recorded_at DESC) AS last_rank
    FROM records
)
SELECT 
    f.entity_id,
    f.category AS initial_category,
    f.recorded_at AS first_date,
    l.category AS final_category,
    l.recorded_at AS last_date
FROM Ranked f
JOIN Ranked l ON f.entity_id = l.entity_id
WHERE f.first_rank = 1 
  AND l.last_rank = 1
  AND f.category != l.category
ORDER BY f.entity_id;
examples.description: State transition detection comparing first and last category values

examples.question: Track status progression showing all transitions for each entity
examples.sql:
WITH WithPrevious AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        status,
        status_date,
        LAG(status) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY status_date) AS prev_status,
        LAG(status_date) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY status_date) AS prev_date
    FROM status_log
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    prev_status AS from_status,
    status AS to_status,
    prev_date AS transition_from,
    status_date AS transition_to,
    DATEDIFF('day', prev_date, status_date) AS days_in_previous_status
FROM WithPrevious
WHERE prev_status IS NOT NULL AND prev_status != status
ORDER BY entity_id, status_date;
examples.description: Track all status transitions using LAG in CTE

examples.question: Compare each location's average to the regional average
examples.sql:
WITH LocationAvg AS (
    SELECT 
        location_id,
        region_id,
        AVG(metric_value) AS location_avg,
        COUNT(*) AS location_count
    FROM metrics
    GROUP BY location_id, region_id
),
RegionAvg AS (
    SELECT 
        region_id,
        AVG(metric_value) AS region_avg,
        COUNT(*) AS region_count
    FROM metrics
    GROUP BY region_id
)
SELECT 
    l.location_id,
    l.region_id,
    ROUND(l.location_avg, 2) AS location_average,
    ROUND(r.region_avg, 2) AS region_average,
    ROUND(l.location_avg - r.region_avg, 2) AS difference,
    CASE 
        WHEN l.location_avg > r.region_avg THEN 'Above'
        WHEN l.location_avg < r.region_avg THEN 'Below'
        ELSE 'Equal'
    END AS comparison
FROM LocationAvg l
JOIN RegionAvg r ON l.region_id = r.region_id
ORDER BY difference DESC;
examples.description: Compare sub-group averages to parent group averages using CTEs

examples.question: Find entities performing above the overall median
examples.sql:
WITH OverallStats AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY score) AS median_score,
        AVG(score) AS mean_score
    FROM performance
),
EntityScores AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        AVG(score) AS entity_avg_score,
        COUNT(*) AS record_count
    FROM performance
    GROUP BY entity_id
)
SELECT 
    e.entity_id,
    ROUND(e.entity_avg_score, 2) AS entity_score,
    ROUND(o.median_score, 2) AS overall_median,
    ROUND(e.entity_avg_score - o.median_score, 2) AS above_median_by,
    e.record_count
FROM EntityScores e
CROSS JOIN OverallStats o
WHERE e.entity_avg_score > o.median_score
ORDER BY above_median_by DESC;
examples.description: Compare entity scores to overall median using PERCENTILE_CONT

examples.question: Get the most recent record for each entity with tie-breaking
examples.sql:
WITH RankedRecords AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        record_id,
        value,
        updated_at,
        created_at,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY entity_id 
            ORDER BY updated_at DESC, created_at DESC, record_id DESC
        ) AS rn
    FROM records
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    record_id,
    value,
    updated_at,
    created_at
FROM RankedRecords
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY entity_id;
examples.description: Deduplication using ROW_NUMBER with multiple tie-breaker columns

examples.question: Select one representative record per group based on priority
examples.sql:
WITH PriorityRanked AS (
    SELECT 
        group_id,
        record_id,
        source,
        value,
        recorded_at,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY group_id 
            ORDER BY 
                CASE source 
                    WHEN 'primary' THEN 1 
                    WHEN 'secondary' THEN 2 
                    ELSE 3 
                END,
                recorded_at DESC,
                record_id DESC
        ) AS priority_rank
    FROM records
    WHERE value IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    group_id,
    record_id,
    source,
    value,
    recorded_at
FROM PriorityRanked
WHERE priority_rank = 1
ORDER BY group_id;
examples.description: Priority-based deduplication with CASE expression in ORDER BY
